title: sideroxylon foetidissimum subsp. gaumeri
intro: Sideroxylon foetidissimum subsp. gaumeri, also known as Gaumer's bull-bay or Gaumer's bullock-bay, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The plant has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and aiding in digestion. Traditional herbal preparations include teas, infusions, and tinctures made from the plant's leaves and bark, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
